# ContextBoundary

> The egress governance specification for AI data flows. Deployment-agnostic, regulatory-mapped, enforceable at the network layer. Defines a five-zone vendor tier architecture, Egress Tier I/II/III classification (Tier I = never leaves the system, Tier III = explicit per-call API escalation), jurisdictional Audit Profiles, and an Endpoint Atlas. Connects to ContextOps as the technical-layer companion. One of three sibling frameworks: ContextOps (organisational governance), ContextBoundary (egress governance), Sthala (sovereign on-prem implementation).

## Core Framework

- [README](README.md): Project overview, positioning, and roadmap
- [FRAMEWORK](FRAMEWORK.md): Core framework specification
- [RATIONALE](RATIONALE.md): Why ContextBoundary exists — the sovereignty gap, regulatory context, and design decisions

## Boundary & Classification

- [Boundary Diagram](boundary-diagram.md): Five-zone boundary diagram and zone definitions
- [Tier Classification](tier-classification.md): Egress Tier I / II / III definitions and routing rules (Tier I = never leaves, Tier III = explicit API escalation)
- [Vendor Tier Matrix](vendor-tier-matrix.md): Vendor placement across zones

## Compliance

- [GDPR Audit Profile](gdpr.md): GDPR jurisdictional profile — lawful basis, data subject rights, retention, controller/processor obligations

## Endpoint Atlas

- [Vendors](vendors.md): Vendor registry classifying AI services by zone and egress tier

## Integration

- [ContextOps Mapping](contextops-mapping.md): Connector specification between ContextBoundary and ContextOps

## Contributing

- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
